statement.p: A rectangles base is {3\ {\rm in}} shorter than four times its height. The rectangles area is {52\ {\rm in^{2}}}. Find this rectangles dimensions.

solution.p: Let the rectangles height be x inches. Then its base is 4x-3 inches.
Its given that the rectangles area is {52\ {\rm in^{2}}}, so we can write and solve the equation:
The polynomial can be factored, so we will first solve this equation by factoring:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] (4x+13)(x-4) \amp =0 \\ 4x+13=0 \amp \text{ or } x-4=0 \\ 4x=-13 \amp \text{ or } x=4 \\ x=\text{a negative number} \amp \text{ or } x=4 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We could also solve the equation by the Quadratic Formula.
Notice that there is no need to calculate the negative result, as a rectangles height cannot be negative. The height is {4\ {\rm in}}.
Recall that the rectangles base is 4x-3 inches, where x is the height. Thus the base is 4 \cdot {4\ {\rm in}} - {3\ {\rm in}}, which is {13\ {\rm in}}.
So the rectangles height is {4\ {\rm in}}, and its base is {13\ {\rm in}}.
